name: Festive Fruitcake w/ Sweet Apricot Glaze
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Light brown sugar; firmly packed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 275°F.
2. Grease an 8-inch springform pan or a 6-cup Bundt pan (see note).
3. In a large bowl, combine pecans, candied pineapple, raisins, dates, candied orange peel, candied cherries, and 1/2 cup flour; toss well to mix.
4. Set aside.
5. In a second large bowl with electric mixer on low speed, beat butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar until light and fluffy.
6. Beat in eggs, orange juice, orange liqueur, grated lemon peel, vanilla, nutmeg, baking soda, and the remaining 1 cup flour until thoroughly blended and smooth.
7. Fold in the reserved fruit mixture.
8. Pour batter into prepared pan.
9. Bake for 2 1/2 hours until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Cool in the pan on a wire rack for 1 hour, then remove cake from the pan to the rack to cool completely.
11. Refrigerate, tightly wrapped in foil, at least 24 hours and up to 3 weeks before serving (see note).
12. At serving time, brush Easy Apricot Glaze over top and sides of cake.
13. Decorate with dried or candied fruit and nuts, if desired.
- Makes 24 servings
- Per serving with glaze without decoration: 248 calories, 3g protein, 8g fat, 42g carbohydrates
- Note: If desired, wrap the cool cake first in cheesecloth soaked in orange-flavored liqueur or brandy before wrapping in foil for storing.
EASY APRICOT GLAZE: In a small saucepan over very low heat, stir 1/2 cup apricot preserves until melted and warm. Source: Redbook, December 1988. - MARY WILSON BWVB02B
